notes: Evals re-run 2026-04-24 via skill-creator simulation (read-only subagent execution — no files modified). 8 subagent runs (4 evals × with_skill + without_skill). Benchmark: with_skill 95% pass rate vs. without_skill 9% (+0.86 delta). Idempotent-rerun improved from 0.00 (2026-04-23) to 0.80 — Step 1.5 guard is now wired in. See evals/workspace/iteration-1/benchmark.md for full stats.

runs.method: skill-creator simulation (read-only general-purpose subagent, no Edit/Write)

assertions.id: checks coverage-report.json exists AND is < 24h old BEFORE any other action
assertions.note: Step 1 first sub-bullet parses generated_at (explicitly not filesystem mtime) and checks < 24h.

assertions.id: checks git status --porcelain is empty and refuses on dirty tree
creates feature branch retrofit/annotate-procest-{today}
creates ghost change named retrofit-annotate-procest-{today} with empty specs/ and one task per REQ
task IDs follow format 'task-N: {capability}#{REQ-NNN} — {title} (retroactive annotation)'
uses AskUserQuestion before any file edits
uses Edit tool (or Write for full-file rewrite) — never sed/awk/python
runs composer phpcs and /hydra-gates after annotation
appends annotation commit SHA to .git-blame-ignore-revs
archives the ghost change via /opsx-archive at the end
PR labels include both retrofit and annotation-only

runs.notes: All 11 assertions now pass. The 2026-04-23 run's ordering nit (Step 0 model-guard before prereqs) is no longer a blocker — the model-guard is now treated as a precondition, not an action.

assertions.id: runs git status --porcelain as part of Step 1 prereq check
assertions.note: Step 1 line 39: 'Working tree is clean (git status --porcelain empty). Refuse dirty trees.'

assertions.id: refuses to proceed when porcelain output is non-empty
cites the Refuse dirty trees guardrail in the refusal message
does NOT create the retrofit branch
does NOT create any ghost-change directory
does NOT modify any file
reads coverage-report.json and parses the generated_at timestamp
assertions.note: Ambiguity from 2026-04-23 fixed — SKILL.md now names generated_at explicitly with 'use generated_at, not filesystem mtime' parenthetical.

assertions.id: compares against current time and detects > 24h staleness
refuses to proceed
recommends running /opsx-coverage-scan procest to refresh
does NOT proceed with annotation

runs.notes: Improved from 0.87 (2026-04-23) to 1.00 — field name is now unambiguous.

assertions.id: greps the code for existing @spec openspec/changes/retrofit-annotate-* tags before annotating
assertions.note: Step 1.5 'Idempotent-rerun guard' now exists and runs between Step 1 prereqs and Step 2.

assertions.id: detects the prior retrofit-annotate-procest-2026-04-01 tags
assertions.note: Prefix match catches dated retrofit-annotate-* tags across all 4 simulated files.

assertions.id: uses AskUserQuestion to present the choice (fresh / reuse / cancel)
assertions.note: Step 1.5 offers Resume/Fresh run/Cancel — three-way choice functionally matches fresh/reuse/cancel.

assertions.id: does NOT silently create a duplicate ghost change
assertions.note: Step 1.5 explicitly: 'Do not proceed silently — a second ghost change mints duplicate tasks and produces a confusing PR.'

assertions.id: if user picks fresh, new dated ghost change only carries NEW Bucket 1 items (not re-annotates already-tagged methods)
assertions.note: Intent stated ('existing tags stay, new ones added for missed files') but the per-file defensive re-check in Step 6 is prose, not a concrete sub-step. Relies on coverage-report.json's annotated bucket being current at scan time.

runs.notes: Improved from hard 0.00 fail (2026-04-23) to 0.80 — Step 1.5 Idempotent-rerun guard was the missing wiring. Remaining 0.20 gap: Step 6 should have a concrete 'grep-before-edit' sub-step for the fresh-run filtering, not just mention 'defensive' skip.

summary.improve_cycle_next: Operationalize the fresh-run filter in Step 6: add a sub-step that re-greps each target file for existing @spec openspec/changes/retrofit-annotate-* tags before the Edit pass and skips already-tagged methods. This would close the last partial on idempotent-rerun and bring the score to 1.00.
